lesson.title: Lesson 57: Back in the Old Country

rights.notes: Migrated from legacy published content; migration does not establish redistribution rights.

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: Did the narrator find his mother's grave?

translation.text: 叙述者找到母亲的坟墓了吗？

segments.text: stopped to let the car cool off and to study the map.

translation.text: 我停下车来，让发动机冷却一下，同时查看地图。

segments.text: I had expected to be near my objective by now, but everything still seemed alien to me.

translation.text: 我原以为现在应该已经接近目的地了，但周围的一切对我仍然十分陌生。

segments.text: I was only five when my father had taken me abroad, and that was eighteen years ago.

translation.text: 父亲带我移居国外时，我只有五岁，而那已经是十八年前的事了。

segments.text: When my mother had died after a tragic accident, he did not quickly recover from the shock and loneliness.

translation.text: 母亲在一次悲惨事故中去世后，父亲久久未能从震惊和孤独中恢复过来。

segments.text: Everything around him was full of her presence continually reopening the wound, so he decided to emigrate.

translation.text: 周围的一切都充满了母亲的影子，不断揭开他的伤口，于是他决定移民。

segments.text: In the New Country, he became absorbed in making a new life for the two of us, so that he gradually ceased to grieve.

translation.text: 到了新国家，他全心投入为我们父子俩建立新生活，渐渐不再悲伤。

segments.text: He did not marry again, and I was brought up without a woman's care, but I lacked for nothing, for he was both father and mother to me.

translation.text: 他没有再婚；虽然我在没有女性照料的环境中长大，却什么都不缺，因为他既当父亲又当母亲。

segments.text: He always meant to go back one day, but not to stay.

translation.text: 他一直打算有一天回去看看，但并不打算留下。

segments.text: His roots and mine had become too firmly embedded in the new land, but he wanted to see the old folk again and to visit my mother's grave.

translation.text: 我和他的根已经深深扎在新土地上，但他想再见见故乡的老人，并去祭扫母亲的坟墓。

segments.text: He became mortally ill a few months before we had planned to go, and, when he knew that he was dying, he made me promise to go on my own.

translation.text: 就在我们计划出发的几个月前，他患上了致命疾病；知道自己将不久于人世时，他要我答应独自回去。

segments.text: I hired a car the day after landing, and bought a comprehensive book of maps, which I found most helpful on the cross-country journey, but which I did not think I should need on the last stage.

translation.text: 抵达后的第二天，我租了一辆车，又买了一本详尽的地图册；穿越乡间时它很有用，但我以为最后一段路不会用到。

segments.text: It was not that I actually remembered anything at all, but my father had described over and over again what we should see at every milestone, after leaving the nearest town, so that I was positive I should recognize

translation.text: 并不是我真的记得什么，而是离开最近的城镇后，每到一个路标父亲都反复描述过我们会看到什么，所以我确信自己会认出

segments.text: it as familiar territory.

translation.text: 这片熟悉的地方。

segments.text: Well, I had been wrong, for I was now lost.

translation.text: 然而我错了，因为现在我迷路了。

segments.text: I looked at the map, and then at the father, I should be looking at farms and cottages in a valley, with the spire of

translation.text: 我看看地图，又看看远方。照父亲的说法，我眼前应该是一座山谷，里面有农场和村舍，还能看见

segments.text: the church of our village showing in the far distance.

translation.text: 我们村教堂的尖顶隐约出现在远处。

segments.text: I could see no valley, no farms, no cottages and no church spire, only a lake.

translation.text: 可是我看不到山谷、农场、村舍或教堂尖顶，只有一个湖。

segments.text: I decided that I must have taken a wrong turning somewhere.

translation.text: 我断定自己一定在某处转错了弯。

segments.text: So I drove back to the town and began to retrace the route, taking frequent glances at the map.

translation.text: 于是我开车回到镇上，开始沿原路重新寻找，并频频查看地图。

segments.text: I landed up at the same corner.

translation.text: 结果我又回到了同一个拐角。

segments.text: The curious thing was that the lake was not marked on the map.

translation.text: 奇怪的是，地图上并没有标出这个湖。

segments.text: I felt as if I had stumbled into a nightmare country, as you sometimes do in dreams.

translation.text: 我觉得自己仿佛误入了梦中常见的噩梦之地。

segments.text: And, as in a nightmare, there was nobody in sight to help me.

translation.text: 也像噩梦一样，四下无人可以帮忙。

segments.text: Fortunately for me, as I was wondering what to do next, there appeared on the horizon a man on horseback, riding in my direction.

translation.text: 幸运的是，正当我不知下一步该怎么办时，地平线上出现了一个骑马人，正朝我这边过来。

segments.text: I waited till he came near, then I asked him the way to our old village.

translation.text: 我等他走近，便向他询问去故乡村庄的路。

segments.text: He said that there was now no village.

translation.text: 他说现在已经没有那个村庄了。

segments.text: I thought he must have misunderstood me, so I repeated its name.

translation.text: 我以为他误解了我的意思，便又说了一遍村名。

segments.text: This time he pointed to the lake.

translation.text: 这一次，他指了指湖。

segments.text: The village no longer existed, because it had been submerged, and all the valley too.

translation.text: 村庄已经不复存在，因为它连同整座山谷都被淹没了。

segments.text: The lake was not a natural one, but a man-made reservoir.

translation.text: 这个湖并非天然湖泊，而是一座人工水库。
